RESPONSE TO SEPARATE STATEMENT AND STATEMENT OF ADDITIONAL DISPUTED FACTS FILED BY DEFENDANT LES JOULINS USA, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 301, NOTICE WAS GIVEN TO THE OTHER PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO ELLIS O'FARRELL PARKING CORPORATION AND CROSS-DEFENDANT RUSSELL KEIL, JR'S MOTION TO AWARD ATTORNEY FEES AND TO AMEND JUDGMENT TO INCLUDE ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS IS DENIED. THERE IS NOT YET A FINAL DETERMINATION AS TO THE PARTIES COMPETING CONTRACT CLAIMS SO THE COURT CANNOT YET DETERMINE THE PREVAILING PARTY IN THIS DISPUTE. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO ELLIS O'FARRELL PARKING CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE REMAINS AN ISSUE FOR TRIAL WHETHER DEFENDANT EXERCISED AN OPTION OR AGREE TO PAY INCREASED RENT FOR THE PERIOD IN QUESTION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE 2 BECAUSE THE COURT HAS DETERMINED THAT DEFENDANT'S FACTS DO NOT ESTABLISH THAT PLAINTIFF BREACHED THE COVENANT, BUT DENIED AS TO THE OTHER AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. THE COURT DOES NOT CONSIDER EVIDENCE FIRST OFFERED ON REPLY. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. J. MICHAEL PHELPS, TEL. NO. 433-3733 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DOUGLAS A. MARSHALL OF THE KAY & MERKLE, LLP, TEL. NO. 357-1200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT LES JOULINS USA, INCORPORATED.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO ELLIS O'FARRELL PARKING CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE REMAINS AN ISSUE FOR TRIAL WHETHER DEFENDANT EXERCISED AN OPTION OR AGREE TO PAY INCREASED RENT FOR THE PERIOD IN QUESTION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE 2 BECAUSE THE COURT HAS DETERMINED THAT DEFENDANT'S FACTS DO NOT ESTABLISH THAT PLAINTIFF BREACHED THE COVENANT, BUT DENIED AS TO THE OTHER AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. THE COURT DOES NOT CONSIDER EVIDENCE FIRST OFFERED ON REPLY. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
